title: limonium retirameum subsp. caralitanum
intro: Limonium retirameum subsp. caralitanum, also known as sea lavender, is a type of flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of sea lavender include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis. Sea lavender can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and calmness.
